commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is maintainer judgment on the diagnostics compatibility tradeoff and final merge or rebase review against current main.; Cleared: No supply-chain or new security-boundary concern was found; the patch reduces user-facing leakage of raw provider errors while retaining internal diagnostics. (sha=5bb04e6997717541b9c0ede4f0abf7784f49f06f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a user-facing assistant-error formatter wrapper and routes embedded lifecycle and reply-payload error text through it, with regression tests for raw canary suppression and aborted-run behavior.

PR surface: Source +41, Tests +59. Total +100 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level. Current main still has a path where unclassified formatter pass-through can become lifecycle error text and an isError reply payload, and the PR's tests exercise that canary path.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is maintainer judgment on the diagnostics compatibility tradeoff and final merge or rebase review against current main.

target.title: fix: suppress raw provider errors in channel delivery
target.body: Fixes #69737. ## Summary This change blocks raw unclassified assistant `errorMessage` text at the user-facing delivery boundary. It adds a shared `formatUserFacingAssistantErrorText(...)` wrapper around the existing assistant error formatter. Classified friendly errors still use the existing sanitizer/classification behavior, but formatter output that is only raw upstream pass-through now falls back to: ```text LLM request failed. ``` Both terminal lifecycle events and reply payload construction now use that shared user-facing wrapper. Raw diagnostic details are not globally deleted, provider-side error objects are not changed, and internal observation fields such as `rawErrorPreview` remain available for maintainers. ## Evidence - Before patch, lifecycle used: ```ts const errorText = (friendlyError || lastAssistant.errorMessage || "LLM request failed.").trim(); ``` - Before patch, reply payloads used: ```ts formatAssistantErrorText(assistantForPayload, ...) ``` and pushed that text as an `isError` reply payload. - After patch, both paths use the shared safe wrapper: ```ts const safeFriendlyError = isRawAssistantErrorPassthrough({ friendlyError, rawError }) ? undefined : friendlyError; return (safeFriendlyError || GENERIC_ASSISTANT_ERROR_TEXT).trim(); ``` - Regression tests: - `handleAgentEnd > suppresses raw assistant error messages in user-facing lifecycle events` - `buildEmbeddedRunPayloads > suppresses raw assistant error messages in user-facing reply payloads` - Synthetic canary: `SECRET_CANARY_69737` - Current HEAD tested: `5bb04e6997717541b9c0ede4f0abf7784f49f06f` - Proof log: `/tmp/openclaw-pr69737-proof.log` - Validation log: `/tmp/openclaw-pr69737-validation.log` ## Real behavior proof - Behavior addressed: terminal assistant errors with unclassified raw upstream `errorMessage` must not send that raw text through user-facing lifecycle or reply payload delivery text. - Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout at HEAD `5bb04e6997717541b9c0ede4f0abf7784f49f06f` in `/home/rev/projects/openclaw-issue-69737-error-boundary`, running the actual `handleAgentEnd` lifecycle function and actual `buildEmbeddedRunPayloads` reply payload builder through `node --import tsx`. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: direct Node/tsx proof imports `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.ts` and `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.ts`, passes a terminal assistant payload with `stopReason: "error"`, `content: []`, and `errorMessage: "SECRET_CANARY_69737"`, then captures user-facing lifecycle event text and reply payload text. - Evidence after fix: terminal output from the direct `node --import tsx` proof command: ```json { "command": "node --import tsx direct handleAgentEnd plus buildEmbeddedRunPayloads canary proof", "head": "5bb04e6997717541b9c0ede4f0abf7784f49f06f", "containsCanaryInUserFacingLifecycleText": false, "containsGenericFallbackInUserFacingLifecycleText": true, "containsCanaryInUserFacingReplyPayloadText": false, "containsGenericFallbackInUserFacingReplyPayloadText": true, "replyPayloads": [ { "text": "LLM request failed.", "isError": true } ], "internalRawPreviewRetainedForMaintainers": true } ``` - Observed result after fix: `SECRET_CANARY_69737` is absent from both user-facing lifecycle text and user-facing reply payload text; both use the generic fallback. Internal diagnostic preview retention still reports true for maintainers. Environment: - Branch: `fix/69737-suppress-raw-error-message` - Head: `5bb04e6997717541b9c0ede4f0abf7784f49f06f` - Channel: live Telegram DM through my existing OpenClaw Telegram bot - Provider route: local OpenAI Responses-compatible mock at `127.0.0.1:8787/v1` - Forced raw provider error canary: `SECRET_CANARY_69737` Steps: 1. Started the current PR checkout locally. 2. Temporarily routed `openai/gpt-5.5` to a local mock provider. 3. The mock provider returned a 500 provider error containing `SECRET_CANARY_69737`. 4. Sent a live Telegram DM to trigger the agent turn. Gateway evidence: ```text [openai-transport] [responses] error provider=openai api=openai-responses model=gpt-5.5 status=500 code=telegram_live_proof_canary type=server_error message=500 SECRET_CANARY_69737 [telegram] embedded run agent end: isError=true model=gpt-5.5 provider=openai error=LLM request failed. rawError=500 SECRET_CANARY_69737 ``` ## Testing ```bash OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.formatassistanterrortext.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors.test.ts ``` Result: ```text Test Files 4 passed (4) Tests 156 passed (156) ``` ```bash git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 --config .oxfmtrc.jsonc src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts ``` Result: ```text All matched files use the correct format. ```
target.author: jason-allen-oneal

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
